message:
refactor(palette): consolidate enhanced features into core model

This refactoring merges the experimental "enhanced" palette implementation
back into the core model, eliminating code duplication while retaining
all the visual improvements.

**Removed Files**
- internal/palette/enhanced_model.go (805 lines)
- internal/palette/enhanced_selector.go (295 lines)

**Enhanced Model (internal/palette/model.go)**
Integrated features from enhanced_model.go:
- Animation system: 50ms tick-based updates for smooth transitions
- Gradient colors: Pre-computed header and list gradients from theme
- Shimmer effects: Animated gradient text for titles and dividers
- Preview pane: Live prompt preview with syntax highlighting
- Visual order mapping: Correct 1-9 quick-select across categories
- Theme integration: Full Catppuccin color palette usage
- Success/error states: Color-coded feedback with agent icons

**Enhanced Selector (internal/palette/selector.go)**
Integrated features from enhanced_selector.go:
- Animation tick for smooth visual updates
- Gradient dividers using styles.GradientDivider()
- Shimmer effect on titles via styles.Shimmer()
- Pulsing "attached" indicator (alternates green/teal)
- Gradient text on selected session name
- Styled help bar with keyboard shortcuts
- Number badges with background styling

**CLI Updates**
- internal/cli/palette.go: Switch from EnhancedModel to Model
- internal/cli/dashboard.go: Switch from RunEnhancedSessionSelector to
  RunSessionSelector

This consolidation reduces ~1100 lines of duplicate code while
maintaining the beautiful animated TUI experience.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-rlp","title":"Implement alert generation and tracking","description":"Generate actionable alerts from state analysis.

## Rationale
Alerts surface problems proactively. Instead of polling and analyzing raw state, the orchestrator gets pre-computed alerts.

## Alert Types
1. **Agent Stuck**: No output for N minutes
2. **Agent Crashed**: Pane process exited
3. **High CPU**: Agent consuming excessive resources
4. **Disk Low**: Running low on disk space
5. **Bead Stale**: In-progress bead with no activity
6. **Mail Backlog**: Many unread messages
7. **Dependency Cycle**: Bead dependency loop detected

## Alert Structure
```go
type Alert struct {
    ID        string    // unique alert ID
    Type      string    // agent_stuck, disk_low, etc.
    Severity  string    // info, warning, error, critical
    Message   string    // human-readable description
    Context   map[string]interface{} // relevant data
    CreatedAt time.Time
    ResolvedAt *time.Time
}
```

## Alert Lifecycle
1. Condition detected → Alert created
2. Alert included in snapshot/health output
3. Condition resolved → Alert marked resolved
4. Resolved alerts pruned after configurable time

## Configurable Thresholds
Via config file:
```toml
[alerts]
agent_stuck_minutes = 5
disk_low_threshold_gb = 5
mail_backlog_threshold = 10

 {"id":"ntm-rm4","title":"Add --json support to ntm status command","description":"Implement JSON output for the status command.

## What This Task Does

Modify the ntm status command to support --json flag for detailed session state.

## JSON Schema

{
  "session": "myproject",
  "exists": true,
  "attached": false,
  "window_count": 1,
  "panes": [
    {
      "index": 0,
      "title": "myproject__cc_1",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "agent_number": 1,
      "dimensions": {"width": 80, "height": 24},
      "pid": 12345,
      "command": "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions",
      "active": true
    }
  ],
  "agents": {
    "claude": 2,
    "codex": 1,
    "gemini": 0
  },
  "working_directory": "/path/to/project",
  "created_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z"
}

## Implementation

1. Import internal/output package
2. Create SessionStatusOutput and PaneInfo structs
3. Modify runStatus to build structured data
4. Include all pane details in JSON
5. Parse agent type from pane titles

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm status myproject --json produces valid JSON
- [ ] All pane information is included
- [ ] Agent type parsed from title
- [ ] Non-existent session returns error JSON
- [ ] Text output unchanged

-{"id":"ntm-sim","title":"Implement --robot-snapshot for unified state query","description":"Core orchestration primitive: single command returns complete system state including all sessions, agent health, bead summary, mail status, and alerts. This is THE most important flag because it eliminates the need for multiple queries.

## Rationale
When orchestrating N agents across M sessions working on P beads with Q mail threads, I currently need separate queries for each dimension. This compounds latency (4-6 tool calls) and burns tokens on redundant parsing. A unified snapshot gives me complete situational awareness in one atomic read.

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "ts": "ISO8601",
  "sessions": [{
    "name": "string",
    "agents": [{
      "pane": "window.pane",
      "type": "claude|codex|gemini|cursor|windsurf|aider",
      "state": "active|idle|stuck|waiting",
      "last_output_age_sec": number,
      "output_tail_lines": number,
      "current_bead": "bead-id|null",
      "pending_mail": number
    }]
  }],
  "beads_summary": {"open": N, "in_progress": N, "blocked": N, "ready": N},
  "mail_unread": number,
  "alerts": ["string"]
}
```

## Key Design Decisions
- Always include everything (no optional fields that require follow-up)
- Use compact field names (abbreviations OK for frequently-accessed data)
- Include computed fields (last_output_age_sec vs raw timestamp)
- Alerts are pre-filtered to actionable items only

## Integration Points
- tmux: session/pane enumeration, output capture
- beads: bd list --json integration  
- agent-mail: inbox count per agent
- pane title parsing for agent type detection","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:50:07.458116-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-y44","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:26.544338-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-rlp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.95212-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-gk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:40.202645-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-l5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:40.264077-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-sim","depends_on_id":"ntm-3n9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:40.32441-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"ntm-sim","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Implementation Architecture
